summary: Implemented the M3 minimal deterministic succession state machine in sim-core and protocol. Character death/incapacity now flows through shared GameCommand paths, vacates held offices/enfeoffments, filters dead relationship endpoints, forms serializable candidate sets from explicit support sources, creates pending succession crises, and resolves peaceful or disputed/regency-style outcomes through stable integer scoring and tie-breakers.

tests_run.result: Expected red state after adding succession tests: new record-character-status / resolve-succession commands and succession query were not implemented yet.
12 test files passed; 70 tests passed, including M3 succession peaceful, vacancy, dead-character rejection, disputed outcome, and canonical candidate-order property coverage.
1 test file passed; 6 tests passed, including M3 succession command/query schema parsing.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic canaries matched; root Vitest 22 files/129 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

Shows this task's protocol/sim-core/test edits and new handoff. Pre-existing modified project coordination files remain: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/active/M3-SUCCESSION-STATE-MACHINE-001.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-SUCCESSION-STATE-MACHINE-001

decisions: R3 protocol/state change: extended existing state.m3 and GameCommand/GameQuery v1 rather than adding a new root package, save-format migration, UI surface, or event narrative system.
Character death/incapacity is represented by explicit alive/incapacitated fields; only sim.record-character-status mutates those fields and commits vacancies/crisis creation.

Candidate support is explicit and serializable through successionCandidateProfiles and copied crisis candidates, with support source kinds kinship, designation, court, military, provincial, suzerain, and foreign.

Succession resolution uses integer BPS totals, a fixed peaceful threshold/margin, and characterId tie-breakers. Disputed outcomes leave the ruler office vacant; peaceful/regency outcomes assign the successor to the trigger office.
Dead or incapacitated characters are unavailable for appointments, enfeoffment, relationship endpoints, and character/office actor authority.
M3 save behavior remains unchanged: requestSaveV1 still refuses worlds containing state.m3.
risks: This is an R3 protocol and WorldState M3 runtime schema change; systems_architect review and later QA review are required before acceptance.
Support weights and peaceful/disputed thresholds are M3-scoped scaffold constants, not final dynasty, diplomacy, or civil-war balance.
The implementation does not add formal historical succession rules, full civil war, full diplomacy, client UI, or M4+ war behavior.
Pre-existing dirty project coordination files were present before implementation and were not reverted or normalized.
requested_action: Review the R3 protocol/state schema, command authority model, deterministic succession transitions, candidate support serialization, and test coverage; then route to qa_reviewer if architecturally acceptable.
